raw_result:
---

## Implementation Plan — Sweeper Service Changes

### Orientation

The sweeper is a single binary (`cmd/sweeper/main.go`) wired to three internal packages. The relevant state:

- `const reconcileEvery = "6h"` — a compile-time string in `main.go:18`, parsed once at startup. This is what becomes the env var.
- `metrics.RecordSweep(n int)` — called once per sweep pass in `runSweep`; it increments `entriesReconciled` but has no sweep-count field.
- Shutdown path (`case <-stop: return`, `main.go:42-44`) — the comment at line 43 explicitly flags that in-flight sweeps are abandoned. The `ctx` created at line 37 is unused (`_ = ctx`).

All three changes converge in `cmd/sweeper/main.go`, which is the only wiring point. Changes are otherwise self-contained in their respective packages.

---

### Change 1 — `SWEEP_INTERVAL` environment variable

**`internal/config/config.go`**

`Config` is already the canonical home for env-sourced runtime config — `QueueDSN` and `StoreDSN` follow the same `envOr` pattern. The interval belongs here by convention, not as a constant in `main`.

- Add field `SweepInterval time.Duration` to `Config`.
- In `Load()`, read `envOr("SWEEP_INTERVAL", "6h")`, then call `time.ParseDuration` on the result.
- Validation rule: the parsed duration must be strictly positive (`<= 0` is rejected). Any unparseable string is also rejected.
- Change `Load()` signature to `(Config, error)` so the caller can fatal on a bad value. This is consistent with how the interval parse error is already handled in `main` — the error surface moves from `main` to `config`, but the fatal still happens in `main`.
- Default: `"6h"` — preserves current behavior when `SWEEP_INTERVAL` is unset.

**`cmd/sweeper/main.go`**

- Delete `const reconcileEvery = "6h"` (line 18).
- Update the `config.Load()` call to capture `(cfg, err)` and `log.Fatalf` on non-nil error.
- Remove the `time.ParseDuration(reconcileEvery)` block (lines 26-29) — `cfg.SweepInterval` is already a `time.Duration`, no further parse needed.
- Pass `cfg.SweepInterval` directly to `time.NewTicker`.

---

### Change 2 — Completed-sweeps counter

**`internal/metrics/metrics.go`**

`RecordSweep` is called exactly once per completed sweep pass from `runSweep`. It is already the increment site for `entriesReconciled`; adding a parallel counter here requires no caller changes.

- Add field `sweepsCompleted atomic.Int64` to `Registry` (alongside the existing `entriesReconciled`).
- In `RecordSweep(n int)`, add `r.sweepsCompleted.Add(1)` — one sweep fired, one counter tick, regardless of how many entries were drained.
- In `Snapshot()`, add `"sweeps_completed": r.sweepsCompleted.Load()` to the returned map.

`RecordSweep`'s signature is unchanged; no callers need updating.

---

### Change 3 — Graceful shutdown

**`cmd/sweeper/main.go`**

The existing shutdown path at line 42 (`case <-stop: return`) returns immediately, abandoning any goroutine launched by `go runSweep(q, st, m)` in the same ticker tick. A `sync.WaitGroup` bracketing each goroutine launch lets `main` drain in-flight work before the process exits.

- Import `sync`.
- Declare `var wg sync.WaitGroup` in `main`.
- Replace the bare `go runSweep(q, st, m)` launch with:
  ```go
  wg.Add(1)
  go func() {
      defer wg.Done()
      runSweep(q, st, m)
  }()
  ```
- In the `case <-stop:` branch, replace bare `return` with `wg.Wait()` followed by `return` — this drains any in-flight sweep before the process exits.
- Remove the unused `ctx := context.Background(); _ = ctx` lines (37-38). Graceful shutdown means "wait for completion," not "cancel on signal," so no context cancellation is needed and the placeholder can be deleted cleanly.

`runSweep`'s signature is unchanged.

---

### Documentation changes

**`docs/architecture.md`**

The "Cadence" section (lines 3-12) hardcodes `6h` in prose and as a diagram comment (`<!-- cadence: 6h -->`). Once the interval is runtime-configurable, those claims are factually incomplete.

- Update prose to say the default cadence is 6h, configurable at runtime via `SWEEP_INTERVAL`.
- Update the diagram comment to `<!-- cadence: SWEEP_INTERVAL (default 6h) -->`.

**`docs/glossary.md`**

The "Sweep cadence" definition (line 3) says "hexahourly interval (6h), since the sweeper currently wakes every 6 hours" — the word "currently" will become misleading and operators need to know the override mechanism.

- Revise the definition to say the cadence defaults to 6h and is set via the `SWEEP_INTERVAL` environment variable (any valid Go duration string).
- Drop the "(6h)" parenthetical that implies a fixed value.

**`README.md`**

Line 3 says "The sweep runs every 6 hours." That claim is user-facing and will be wrong.

- Change to: "The sweep runs every 6 hours by default; set `SWEEP_INTERVAL` to override (e.g. `SWEEP_INTERVAL=1h`)."

**`docs/runbook.md`**

The runbook encodes the 6h cadence in three places ("six-hourly reconciliation window", "hexahourly cadence", "three consecutive six-hourly wakes"). An operator running a non-default interval will miscalibrate escalation timers against prose that asserts a specific period.

- Replace each fixed-period reference with "the configured cadence (default 6h)" in the relevant sentences.
- The escalation rule ("three consecutive wakes") remains valid structurally — just replace "six-hourly wakes" with "cadence-interval wakes."

---

### Files touched — summary

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `internal/config/config.go` | Add `SweepInterval time.Duration`; parse + validate `SWEEP_INTERVAL` env var; change `Load` to return `(Config, error)` |
| `internal/metrics/metrics.go` | Add `sweepsCompleted atomic.Int64`; increment in `RecordSweep`; expose in `Snapshot` |
| `cmd/sweeper/main.go` | Remove compile-time constant; use `cfg.SweepInterval`; add `sync.WaitGroup` for graceful drain; remove unused `ctx` |
| `docs/architecture.md` | Replace hardcoded cadence with "default 6h, configurable via `SWEEP_INTERVAL`" |
| `docs/glossary.md` | Update "Sweep cadence" definition |
| `README.md` | Update "every 6 hours" claim to note env var |
| `docs/runbook.md` | Replace hexahourly/six-hourly references with "configured cadence (default 6h)" |

No new files. `internal/queue` and `internal/store` are not touched — all changes are in wiring, config, and metrics.

---

PLEASE-STEP3-PROBE-MARKER-ab455984
